answer:
**Manu (Manusmṛti 9.103ff):** Sons are the primary heirs to the father.[^1] In their absence ("a sonless man"), the father and brothers inherit the property.[^2] The mother (of the deceased) then inherits; if she too is gone, the paternal grandmother does.[^3] The nearest *sapinda* (agnate) takes precedence otherwise, followed by *sakulya*, teacher, or pupil; learned Brāhmaṇas as ultimate default (excluding the king for a Brāhmaṇa).[^4] A daughter inherits only her mother's *yautaka* (personal property) if unmarried; otherwise, her son (*putrātmajā*) takes the estate of a sonless maternal grandfather.[^5] An "appointed daughter" (*putrikā*, declared pre-mortem to continue the line) or her son may inherit to perform ancestral rites, but a natural son supersedes her with equal shares.[^6] The widow (*wife*) has no direct inheritance right; she may receive maintenance but not the estate, which reverts to paternal kin to ensure *pitṛ* rites.

**Yājñavalkya (Yājñavalkya Smṛti 2.114ff):** Sons share equally post-parents' death (or per father's partition).[^7] For a sonless man, heirs succeed sequentially: (1) *wife*, (2) *daughters*, (3) parents, (4) brothers + their sons, (5) same-*gotra* kinsmen, (6) cognates, (7) pupil, (8) fellow-student.[^8] The heir (e.g., of a sonless man) must discharge debts.[^9] A reunited coparcener or full brother inherits preferentially over half-brothers.[^10] Impotent/outcaste sons get maintenance, not shares; their legitimate sons do.[^11] Childless wives of good conduct get maintenance.[^12] *Strīdhana* (woman's property) goes to husband (approved marriages), daughters (if issue), or kin (disapproved forms).[^13]

**Comparison:** Yājñavalkya favors **the widow** (first heir) and **the daughter** (immediate next). Manu favors neither directly—widow gets no share (maintenance only); daughter only via *putrikā* appointment or her son—prioritizing paternal male kin (*sapindas*).

**Principles:** Manu stresses agnatic (*sapinda*) continuity for *pitṛmedha* (ancestral offerings via male line/substitutes), excluding wife/daughter to prevent "line extinction."[^14] Yājñavalkya prioritizes nuclear family (*wife/daughters* first), broadening to agnates only later, reflecting a more immediate household focus.[^15]

[^1]: Manusmṛti, ch. 9, [full chapter](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/09/translation/).

[^2]: Manusmṛti, ch. 9, [p-29f6da](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/09/translation/#p-29f6da).

[^3]: Manusmṛti, ch. 9, [para on Mn_9.217](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/09/translation/#p-on-9.217). (Note: Exact p-id from full text; mother of deceased.)

[^4]: Manusmṛti, ch. 9, [p-nearest sapinda](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/09/translation/#p-9.187).

[^5]: Manusmṛti, ch. 9, [p-9.131](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/09/translation/#p-9.131).

[^6]: Manusmṛti, ch. 9, [p-9.127-136](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/09/translation/#p-9.127).

[^7]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [p-24be00](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-24be00) (Yj_2.117).

[^8]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [p-807a82](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-807a82) to [p-c8fbd0](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-c8fbd0) (Yj_2.135-136).

[^9]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [p-c1fdec](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-c1fdec) (Yj_2.51).

[^10]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [p-da3901](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-da3901) (Yj_2.138).

[^11]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [p-e1cde8](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-e1cde8) (Yj_2.141).

[^12]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [p-44ed2a](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-44ed2a) (Yj_2.142).

[^13]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [p-418cd5](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-418cd5)ff. (Yj_2.143ff.).

[^14]: Manusmṛti, ch. 9, [p-9.138](/works/unknown-manusmrti-347b76/09/translation/#p-9.138) (son delivers from *put* hell).

[^15]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, ch. 2, [full inheritance section](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/02/translation/#p-4d6fe3) (starts Yj_2.114).
